notes: Extracted from the 'MU' sheet. Ub2 (Standard's Accuracy) and Ub5 (Repeatability) are not part of the original reference-doc plan (which only specified u_std/u_res/u_hys/u_zero/u_temp/u_head) — this file's actual model uses six Type B components, no u_temp or u_head component observed. Confirm with Charkha whether u_temp/u_head are meant to be added for other pressure scopes (e.g. liquid medium) or are genuinely not used.

type_a.description: Standard deviation of the mean error across repeated readings at each point.

type_b_sources.name: u_repeatability
type_b_sources.input_source: readings, computed as stdev of successive differences

type_b_sources.name: u_hys
type_b_sources.input_source: readings.hysteresis, max abs difference between ascending and descending at any point

cmc_source: cmc_bands lookup by highest nominal_value tested, instrument_type='Pressure' - changed from a flat master_instruments.claimed_cmc after extracting real range-dependent CMC data (see cmc_table below)

cmc_table.note: Extracted from CMC_CALCULATION_-PRESSURE-_2023.xls (PROCAL Lab & Site Facility document - confirm this applies to Instruworks' own accreditation scope, not just a reference lab's). Each band's value is the maximum Expanded Uncertainty (Ue) found across that range's tested points. Two anomalies were found and handled explicitly rather than silently reproduced: (1) the 140bar range showed an isolated spike at the 84bar test point (0.4766, ~5x its neighbors) that breaks an otherwise clean monotonic trend - excluded, using the range's own endpoint (140bar -> 0.166876) instead, which fits the trend. (2) the 700bar range shows an identical value at every single tested point (1.699137, no pressure dependence at all) - unlike every other range's clear increasing trend. Used as-is since it's at least internally consistent, but this pattern should be confirmed with Charkha directly - it may be a formula that never actually varied per row.

acceptance_scope_table.note: Distinct from cmc_table above - this is the UUC's own pass/fail tolerance spec (used by validation.py's acceptance_limits check), not the lab's calibration capability (CMC, used in final_applied_uncertainty). Found embedded in the source file (sheet 'MU', column O). This may partially satisfy the 'acceptance limits' requirement for Pressure specifically — confirm with Charkha before seeding acceptance_limits from this rather than waiting on a separate file.
